data_structures.aggregation: flat_array[6] per thread, indexed by (returnflag_code * 2 + linestatus_code); each slot holds {sum_qty, sum_base_price, sum_disc_price, sum_charge, sum_qty_cnt, sum_ep_cnt, sum_disc_cnt, row_count} as int64_t accumulators
data_structures.zone_map: mmap indexes/lineitem_l_shipdate_zonemap.bin; layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=600] then per block [int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t block_size]; skip block if min > 10471; accept full block without per-row check if max <= 10471

parallelism.comment: 1 block = 1 morsel; 600 blocks / 64 threads = ~10 blocks per thread; threads pull morsels via atomic fetch_add on block counter
parallelism.aggregation_merge: sequential merge of up to 64 flat_array[6] after parallel scan phase — trivially cheap (6 slots)

index_usage.purpose: skip trailing blocks where min_val > 10471; data sorted by l_shipdate enables contiguous range skip at end of file (~22 of 600 blocks skippable); full-block fast-path when max_val <= 10471 (omit per-row predicate check)

column_decoding.l_shipdate: plain int32_t, days_since_epoch — compare directly to threshold 10471
column_decoding.l_returnflag: dictionary-encoded int32_t (3 entries); use raw code (0, 1, 2) as array dimension 0 directly; load l_returnflag_dict.txt only to map codes to chars for final output
column_decoding.l_linestatus: dictionary-encoded int32_t (2 entries); use raw code (0, 1) as array dimension 1 directly; load l_linestatus_dict.txt only to map codes to chars for final output
column_decoding.l_quantity: plain int64_t, scale_factor=100 — accumulate as integer, divide at output
column_decoding.l_extendedprice: plain int64_t, scale_factor=100 — accumulate as integer, divide at output
column_decoding.l_discount: plain int64_t, scale_factor=100 — use integer arithmetic for (1-discount) and (1+tax) using scaled values to avoid FP in hot loop

physical_plan.arithmetic_note: Compute sum_disc_price as SUM(extendedprice * (100 - discount)) / 10000, sum_charge as SUM(extendedprice * (100 - discount) * (100 + tax)) / 1000000, all in integer arithmetic inside the hot loop to avoid FP conversion overhead per row

estimated_cost.notes: ~578 of 600 blocks processed (~57.8M rows scanned); zone map skips ~22 trailing blocks. 6-slot flat array fits entirely in L1 cache per thread — zero hash overhead. 64-thread morsel-driven scan amortizes HDD sequential read cost. Thread-local merge is O(64*6) = trivial. Target matches Umbra/DuckDB at 83ms.
